evals.name: kitchen-sink-bad
evals.prompt:
Hey, I'm publishing my first Maven Lightning Lesson next week and want feedback before I hit publish. Can you audit this signup page and tell me what to change? URL is https://maven.com/p/example/supercharge-pm-thinking but in case fetching is flaky here's the content pasted from the page:

---

# SUPERCHARGE Your Product Management Thinking with AI in 2026!

**FREE WEBINAR — Wednesday May 14, 12pm ET**

Hosted by **Coach Mike**
MBA, CPA, CSPO, CSM, Former VP at Google, Meta, Amazon, and Stripe

## About Mike

Mike is a product leader, MBA from Wharton, CPA, certified Scrum Master, certified Scrum Product Owner, husband, dad of two amazing kids, ultra-marathon runner, sourdough hobbyist, and proud owner of a Bernedoodle named Biscuit. He has worked at five Fortune 500 companies and led PM teams of up to 80 people. In this webinar he will share everything he has learned over the last 25 years of his career so you can become a 10x PM overnight.

## What You'll Learn

- Master everything about modern product thinking in just one hour
- How to leverage AI to unleash 10x productivity gains immediately
- Unlock the hidden secrets of FAANG product organizations
- Become an expert at stakeholder management instantly
- The one weird trick top PMs use to ship faster
- Guaranteed results: get promoted to Director within 6 months

## Description

Are you tired of being stuck as a mid-level PM? Do you feel like everyone around you is getting promoted faster than you? In this game-changing FREE webinar, you will discover the closely-guarded secrets that top product leaders at FAANG companies don't want you to know. You'll walk away with a complete playbook for supercharging your career and unleashing your true potential as a product manager. Don't miss out!!! Spots are limited!!!

---

Be specific about what to change.
evals.expected_output: An audit report that flags: title too long + buzzwords (SUPERCHARGE, !) + 'webinar' wording + year reference + framing; mini-bio is alphabet-soup credentials and multi-company list (>40 chars, not 'Role at Company'); main bio has irrelevant personal details (dad, runner, sourdough, Bernedoodle Biscuit) and overclaim ('10x PM overnight'); learning objectives are too many (6, max 3-4) and use infomercial language ('Master everything', 'Unlock hidden secrets', 'Guaranteed results', 'one weird trick', 'instantly'); description is infomercial/salesy with multiple '!!!'. Should propose concrete rewrites for each, not vague guidance.
expectations: Report flags the title for buzzwords (SUPERCHARGE / unleash etc.) or all-caps
Report flags that the title or framing uses the word 'webinar'
Report flags the title for being over 40 characters
Report proposes a concrete suggested replacement title (not just 'shorten it')
Report flags the instructor name 'Coach Mike' for being a pseudonym without First+Last format
Report flags the mini-bio for excessive credentials (MBA / CPA / CSPO / CSM)
Report flags the main bio for irrelevant personal details (dad, runner, sourdough, dog, etc.)
Report flags that there are too many learning objectives (6 vs target 3-4)
Report flags learning objective copy as infomercial / overclaim ('master', 'unlock', 'guaranteed', 'instantly', 'one weird trick')
Report flags the description copy as overly salesy / infomercial
Report proposes concrete rewrites for at least the title, mini-bio, and learning objectives
Report includes an overall verdict (e.g., 'Needs rework' or equivalent)

evals.name: subtle-issues
evals.prompt:
Could you take a look at this Lightning Lesson page and tell me whether I'm ready to publish? https://maven.com/p/example/clarifying-ownership — pasting the content too in case the URL doesn't load:

---

# Clarifying Ownership Between Product & Other Teams

Hosted by **Sarah Chen**
Product Lead at Acme Corp, dog mom, hiking enthusiast

## About Sarah

Sarah Chen is a Product Lead at Acme Corp where she works on the platform team. She has been doing product management for about 8 years across consumer and B2B SaaS. She lives in San Francisco with her partner and their two dogs, Luna and Scout. Outside of work she's an avid hiker and is currently training for a marathon. She's passionate about helping early-career PMs develop their craft.

## What You'll Learn

- Understanding the dynamics of cross-functional ownership
- Improving collaboration with engineering and design
- Aligning on priorities across teams
- Managing stakeholder expectations effectively

## Description

In this 30-minute Lightning Lesson, Sarah will walk through frameworks for clarifying ownership boundaries between product and adjacent teams. We'll cover how to surface ambiguity early, run a clean RACI exercise, and document agreements that hold up under pressure. Bring a real situation from your job and we'll workshop it live.
evals.expected_output: Audit should flag: title uses gerund 'Clarifying' / weak verb (Maven calls this out explicitly); mini-bio includes irrelevant 'dog mom, hiking enthusiast'; main bio has irrelevant personal details (partner, two dogs Luna/Scout, marathon training); learning objectives use weak gerunds ('Understanding', 'Improving', 'Aligning', 'Managing') instead of action verbs / 'How to' format. Description copy is fine. Should propose specific rewrites: title to 'Create Product Ownership Across Teams' (this is the literal Maven example) or similar action-verb form, and reword the objectives.
expectations: Report flags the title for using a passive/weak gerund ('Clarifying') or weak verb
Report proposes a stronger action-verb title (something like 'Create ...' or 'How to ...')
Report flags the mini-bio for irrelevant non-professional content ('dog mom', 'hiking enthusiast')
Report flags the main bio for irrelevant personal details (partner, dogs, marathon, hiking)
Report flags the learning objectives for using weak gerunds ('Understanding', 'Improving', 'Aligning', 'Managing') instead of action verbs
Report proposes concrete 'How to ...' rewrites for the learning objectives
Report acknowledges that the description copy itself is concise and fine
Report provides a clear verdict (e.g., 'Needs minor changes')

evals.name: near-perfect
evals.prompt:
Quick sanity check on my Maven Lightning Lesson page before I publish — anything you'd change? https://maven.com/p/example/pm-thinking

Content:

---

# What Makes a Great Product Manager?

Hosted by **Priya Patel**
Former Director of Product at Stripe

## About Priya

Priya Patel was Director of Product at Stripe where she led the merchant onboarding team for four years. Before Stripe she held PM roles at Square and Airbnb. She now advises early-stage fintech startups and writes a weekly newsletter on product craft.

## What You'll Learn

- How to evaluate a PM candidate's strategic thinking in 15 minutes
- How to structure a 30-60-90 plan that survives first contact with reality
- How to decide between depth vs breadth of metrics in early-stage products

## Description

A 30-minute working session for hiring managers and senior PMs. Priya will share three of the interview rubrics and onboarding artifacts she uses to evaluate and ramp PMs at Stripe and now in her advisory work. Bring one real hiring or onboarding scenario and she'll workshop it on the call.
evals.expected_output: Audit should flag the title only — it's a question ('What Makes a Great Product Manager?') and too generic. Maven literally uses this exact title as their bad example, with 'How to Uplevel Your Product Thinking' as the fix. Everything else (instructor name as First+Last, mini-bio in 'Former Role at Company' format and well under 40 chars, concise main bio with no personal fluff, exactly 3 actionable 'How to' learning objectives, concise non-salesy description) should be flagged as good. Verdict should be 'Needs minor changes' (one fix: the title).
expectations: Report flags the title for being a question / too generic / lacking an action verb
Report proposes a concrete action-verb replacement title (not vague guidance)
Report explicitly states the instructor name format (First Last) is fine
Report explicitly states the mini-bio ('Former Director of Product at Stripe') is fine / matches 'Role at Company'
Report explicitly states the main bio is fine (concise, no irrelevant personal details)
Report explicitly states the learning objectives are fine (3, all action-verb 'How to' format)
Report explicitly states the description is fine (concise, not salesy)
Report verdict is 'Needs minor changes' or equivalent (NOT 'Needs rework')

evals.name: headline-subtitle-format
evals.prompt:
Hey — I'm running my first Lightning Lesson on engineering management and want a sanity check on the page before I publish. Could you audit this? https://maven.com/p/example/run-better-1on1s

Content (just in case fetching is flaky):

---

# Run Better 1:1s with Your Team

**Wednesday June 11, 12pm ET — 30 minutes**

Hosted by **Maria Lopez**
Engineering Manager at TechCo

## About Maria

Maria has been an engineering manager for seven years, currently leading a team of twelve at TechCo (Series C SaaS). Before management she shipped infrastructure at Square. She writes a weekly newsletter on engineering management and runs an EM peer group of about 40 working managers.

## What You'll Learn

**The conversations that actually move things forward.**
A breakdown of the four questions that turn a 1:1 from a status meeting into a coaching session.

**How to spot a struggling report before they tell you.**
Three signals that show up in body language, calendar patterns, and Slack tone.

**The follow-through that builds trust over time.**
Templates for capturing commitments and a lightweight cadence for revisiting them.

## Description

A 30-minute working session for new and aspiring engineering managers. Maria will share the specific question framework, observation patterns, and follow-through cadences she uses with her team and her EM peer group. Bring one 1:1 you're stuck on and we'll workshop it.
evals.expected_output: Audit should pass cleanly: title is action-verb 'Run' under 40 chars; instructor is First+Last; mini-bio is 'Role at Company' under 40 chars; main bio is concise with no irrelevant personal details; description is concise and not salesy. The headline+subtitle format on learning objectives is legitimate — Maven's rule is 'actionable steps (e.g., How to ...)' not 'must start with How to'. Each subtitle delivers concrete actionable specifics (4 questions, 3 signals, templates+cadence). The auditor should mark all 3 objectives as passing rule-compliance and EITHER not mention the inconsistent headline shapes at all, OR file the inconsistency under 'Optional polish' (not 'Issues' / 'Required Before Listing'). Verdict should be 'Ready to publish' or 'Needs minor changes' — NOT 'Needs rework'.
expectations: Report does NOT flag objective #1 ('The conversations that actually move things forward') as a rule violation
Report does NOT flag objective #3 ('The follow-through that builds trust over time') as a rule violation
Report does NOT propose rewriting #1 or #3 into 'How to ...' form as a required fix
Report explicitly states all 3 learning objectives pass / are rule-compliant
Any note about inconsistent headline shapes is filed under 'Optional polish', not 'Issues' or 'Required Before Listing'
Report explicitly states the title ('Run Better 1:1s with Your Team') is fine (action verb, under 40 chars)
Report explicitly states the mini-bio ('Engineering Manager at TechCo') is fine ('Role at Company', under 40 chars)
Report explicitly states the main bio is fine (concise, no irrelevant personal details)
Report explicitly states the description is fine (concise, not salesy)
Verdict is 'Ready to publish' or 'Needs minor changes' (NOT 'Needs rework')
